writetfl 0.0.0.9000

Specify display properties for a single table column

tfl_colspec.Rd

Creates a column specification object for use with tfl_table(). All arguments are optional — unspecified properties fall back to the corresponding flat argument in tfl_table(), or to type-based defaults.

You can use tfl_colspec() for fine-grained per-column control, or use the flat arguments of tfl_table() (col_widths, col_labels, col_align, wrap_cols) for simpler cases. When both are provided for the same column, the tfl_colspec() entry takes priority.

Usage

tfl_colspec(
  col,
  label = NULL,
  width = NULL,
  align = NULL,
  wrap = FALSE,
  gp = NULL
)

Arguments

col

Character scalar. Column name in the data frame passed to tfl_table().

label

Character scalar or NULL. Display label for the column header. NULL uses the column name. Use "\n" for multiline headers.

width

A unit object (e.g. unit(1.5, "inches")) or a plain positive numeric (treated as a relative weight; columns with relative weights are scaled to fill available width after fixed-width columns are placed). NULL triggers content-based auto-sizing.

align

Character scalar: "left", "right", or "centre". NULL defaults to "right" for numeric columns and "left" otherwise.

wrap

Logical. Whether this column is eligible for word-wrapping when total column widths exceed available width.

gp

A gpar() object to override tfl_table()'s gp$group_col for this specific column. Only valid for row-header (group) columns; an error is raised if applied to a data column.

Value

An object of class "tfl_colspec".

See also

tfl_table()

On this page
